finding.contract_claim: Parameter `error` is declared in the signature of log(message, error=False) (src/app/launcher.py line 1) as an accepted input that callers may pass.
finding.observed_behavior: The attributed call site, log("ok") in src/app/cli.py, does not pass `error`, so the parameter appears to always take its default False. `log` is a common name with high fan-out: src/noise.py defines an unrelated log(message) that is called locally.
finding.triage_reasoning: The `error` parameter is actively used inside the function body: `return f"{message}:{error}"`. A parameter that directly shapes the return value is not dead — it changes observable behavior when a caller passes `error=True`. The reference sweep covered only 3 files, which is insufficient to conclude no caller ever passes the flag. The function is a public utility with a default that makes the parameter optional but not unused. No gap exists.
